summary: I was born in Philadelphia on October 23rd, 1904. My father owned a dress factory there, which he and his three brothers started as first-generation immigrants. The business became very successful and was a significant part of the garment industry in Philadelphia for many years. Although it eventually went public and the original owners stepped back, Philadelphia was the place where our family built our legacy.

My mother was from Russia, which connects me to my family's immigrant roots. My father and his three brothers, also immigrants, started our dress factory business after coming from Russia. Their background as first-generation immigrants from Russia shaped the way they worked hard to establish a successful company in Philadelphia. Russia is an important part of my family's history and identity.
